name: Aji Verde
procedure: 1. Combine lime juice, mayo, garlic, salt, olive oil, jalapenos, half cilantro. Blend.
2. Add other half of cilantro.
3. Blend on low.
notes: PDF p25 — blend in two stages; half cilantro each time

name: Alabama White Sauce
procedure: 1. Whisk ingredients thoroughly.
2. If brown sugar solidified, dissolve in ACV first.
notes: PDF p26 — mayo + ACV base; dissolve brown sugar in ACV if solidified

name: Bacon Jam
procedure: 1. Render bacon on flat top until lightly crispy.
2. Season with lariat rub.
3. Add onions, cook on medium heat until caramelized.
4. Add brown sugar to melt.
5. Add orange juice and reduce to jammy.
notes: Render bacon crisp — reduce OJ to jammy

name: Beer Batter
procedure: 1. Disperse vodka/stout mix into bowl.
2. Incorporate flour gradually (DRY to WET).
3. Whisk until smooth, runs off whisk.
notes: PDF p21 — wet portion only (guinness + vodka); combine with beer_flour dry mix at service

name: Beer Flour
procedure: Disperse vodka/stout mix into bowl.
Incorporate equal parts flour gradually into bowl (DRY YOUR WET).
Whisk until consistency is smooth and runs off whisk (no clumping/dripping).
notes: PDF p21 — dry mix combined with beer_batter wet portion at service

name: QB Recipe (Birria)
procedure: 1. Dissolve achiote in chicken stock.
2. Trim beef of excessive fat.
3. Season beef with birria seasoning.
4. Sear on all sides.
5. Split into 2 deep hotel pans, cook 8-12 hrs at 225°F.
6. Remove beef. Blend and strain through chinois.
7. Add water to consommé if needed.
notes: PDF p46 — beef cheeks; braise 8-12hr at 225F fatty side up; strain consomme through chinois; birria_seasoning = qb_seasoning sub-recipe

name: Black Bean & Corn Succotash
procedure: 1. Combine all ingredients and mix.
notes: Chill 1 hour before service

name: Blackened Tomato Salsa
procedure: 1. Blacken tomatoes on char grill.
2. Blacken halved onions and jalapenos in salamander.
3. Combine all ingredients in 22 qt cambro or large pot.
4. Bur mix thoroughly.
notes: PDF p8 — blacken tomatoes on char grill; halved onions + jalapenos in salamander; bur mix

name: Buttermilk Brine
procedure: 1. Combine all ingredients in a cambro and whisk.
notes: 12 qt of brined chicken — 12-24hr soak

name: Chicken Confit
procedure: 1. French chicken legs.
2. Line shallow hotel pan with chicken legs.
3. Season both sides w/ green salt.
4. Fill pan with olive oil until submerged.
5. Cover with foil, bake @225°F for 3 hours.
6. Remove and refrigerate immediately.
notes: French legs — confit in EVOO 225F 3hr

name: Chicken Flour
procedure: 1. Combine and mix thoroughly.
notes: Dry dredge before beer batter

name: Chicken Jus
procedure: 1. Heat rondeau; add butter and wings. Cook until colored.
2. Add mirepoix, scrape fond continuously.
3. Once vegetables brown, deglaze with white wine.
4. Add garlic, cook until fond forms, deglaze again.
5. Add remaining stock, reduce by ⅓ (approx. 2 hours).
6. Pour through chinois. Skim oils from top.
notes: Reduce by 1/3 approx 2 hours — strain through chinois

name: Chimichurri
procedure: 1. Pick herbs and finely chop.
2. Mix with peppers, lemon juice and oil.
notes: WORKING RECIPE — please refine and record

name: Chipotle Aioli
procedure: 1. Combine ingredients and blend thoroughly.
notes: Adobo puree + lime

name: Cobb Dressing
procedure: 1. Combine half blue cheese and all other ingredients. Blend on high.
2. Add remaining blue cheese, blend on low.
notes: Blend half bleu cheese high — remaining on low

name: Coleslaw
procedure: 1. Whisk mayo, relish, buttermilk, horseradish, mustard, vinegar, sugar.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Combine cabbage, scallion greens, scallion whites.
4. Pour dressing over slaw, toss well.
5. Adjust seasoning. Store refrigerated.
notes: Pickle relish + horseradish dressing — Hillstone style

name: Corn Bread
procedure: 1. Thoroughly whisk together in a large mixing bowl.
2. Spray cake pans and distribute evenly.
3. Bake at 500°F for 1hr 30min.
4. Remove from oven, cool, and wrap.
notes: Bake 500F 1hr 30min — brown butter

name: Corndog Batter
procedure: 1. Combine all ingredients and whisk.

name: Fish Brine
procedure: 1. Combine ingredients.
2. Marinade for 7 minutes.
3. Drain and cut.
notes: 7 minute marinade only

name: French Toast Batter

notes: Eggs + milk + cinnamon + vanilla + nutmeg

name: Green Chile
procedure: 1. Create roux with bacon grease & flour.
2. Cook pork in oil until water is gone.
3. Add onions, cook 10 min.
4. Add garlic puree, cook 5 min.
5. Add spices and chicken base, cook 10 min.
6. Add tomatillo and chilis, mix well.
7. Add water, cook 40-60 min until pork softens.
8. Add roux, cook 20 more minutes.
notes: PDF p16 — braise 40-60 min until pork softens; add roux (bacon grease + flour) to finish

name: Herbal Butter
procedure: 1. Pick herbs and chop.
2. Stir herbs into butter thoroughly.
notes: Parsley thyme rosemary tarragon compound butter

name: Honey Butter
procedure: 1. Mix honey and butter.

name: Honey Mustard

name: Mesa Melt – Southwest Beer Cheese
procedure: 1. Prepare the Roux: In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook
for 1–2 minutes, until lightly golden and aromatic.
2. Incorporate Beer: Slowly pour in the beer while whisking continuously. Allow it to foam, then simmer for
1–2 minutes until slightly thickened.
3. Add Milk: Whisk in the milk or half-and-half, stirring until smooth and gently thickened.
4. Melt the Cheeses: Reduce heat to medium-low. Add cheddar, pepper jack, and cream cheese gradually,
whisking until fully melted and smooth.
5. Integrate Flavors: Stir in roasted Hatch chiles, chipotle, smoked paprika, cumin, garlic powder, onion
powder, and salt. Mix thoroughly to incorporate.
6. Adjust Consistency: For a thinner sauce, add small increments of milk or beer until desired texture is
reached. For thicker consistency, continue simmering gently.
7. Hold Warm for Service: Keep over low heat or in a warm holding unit until ready for use. Avoid boiling
once cheese is added to maintain a smooth texture.
notes: Roux + Modelo + cheddar + pepper jack + cream cheese + hatch chile + chipotle

name: Queso / Mac Sauce

2. Shred cheese blocks in Robocoupe.
3. Slowly incorporate shredded cheese into boiling pot, stir until melted.
4. Transfer into 22 Qt. cambro and insert ice wand.

name: Three Cheese Grilled Cheese
procedure: 1. Cream Cheese Spread: Blend cream cheese, cheddar, cream, salt smooth.
2. Garlic Spread: Stir mayo, garlic, salt in bowl.
3. Lay out bread. 2 cheddar on 4 slices, 2 provolone on rest.
4. Spoon cream cheese spread on provolone. Press sides together.
5. Heat skillet medium 5 min. Brush both sides with garlic spread.
6. Grill 2 min each side until melted and golden.
notes: Cream cheese spread + garlic spread + cheddar/provolone

name: Tomatillo Salsa
procedure: 1. Roast onions, jalapenos, garlic, tomatillos until charred.
2. Combine with other ingredients and blend.
3. Adjust salt & pepper to taste.
notes: Roast in salamander — blend with avocado

name: Tomato Confit
notes: In-house — 1/3 hotel pan 6-in deep; cherry tomatoes + thyme + 100g garlic + EVOO to cover

name: Tomato Soup
procedure: In a heavy pot or rondeau over medium heat, melt butter.
Add onions and a pinch of salt; cook 8-10 minutes, stirring, until translucent and tender.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds more, just until fragrant.
Add white wine and thyme sprigs.
Simmer 5-6 minutes, reducing the wine by about one-third.
Stir in stock.
Simmer gently for 15-20 minutes.
Stir in heavy cream; reduce heat to low. Season with salt and pepper.
notes: Creamy tomato soup base — butter + onion + garlic + wine + stock + cream
